hero.title: Find the piano that's right for you
hero.subtitle: Your accredited source of truth for the whole piano-owning journey — impartial guidance for buyers and owners, across every leading brand and vintage instrument.
hero.quote: “The advice I wish I'd had before my first piano.”

trust.t1Desc: We work for you, not a showroom. No brand quota, no pressure — only the best fit for your playing, space and budget.
trust.t2Desc: Guidance grounded in professional standards, decades of hands-on experience and an independent technician network.
trust.t3Desc: Clear, jargon-free answers on brands, models, value and condition — so you can decide with confidence.
trust.t4Desc: From first purchase to tuning, restoration, valuation and resale — one trusted partner for life.

intro.title: An accredited home for anyone on a piano-owning journey.
intro.lead: Buying or living with a piano should not mean navigating sales pressure and conflicting opinions alone.
intro.text: Piano Compass is an independent advisory that stands beside you — not behind a sales counter. We translate a complex market into clear guidance, help you shortlist and play-test the right instruments, arrange independent inspection, and support you for as long as you own the piano. We advise across Feurich, every leading maker, and carefully verified vintage instruments.

finder.title: A consultative shortlist in four questions
finder.q1hint: This shapes touch, tone and how much instrument you need.
finder.q2hint: Room size and acoustics guide the right size and type.
finder.q2bs: A room to fill, not overwhelm
finder.q2cs: Room for a grand to breathe
finder.q3: New, vintage, or open to both?
finder.q3hint: Vintage can offer remarkable value — when properly verified.
finder.q4hint: We'll weight your shortlist toward this priority.
finder.resultCta: Book a consultation to refine this

finderResult.grand: A grand piano sized to the room
finderResult.grandOrTall: A tall upright or compact grand
finderResult.baseText: Based on your answers, here is a sensible starting point. A short consultation will refine it into a specific shortlist of makes and models.
finderResult.vintageAdd: A verified vintage instrument could offer excellent value here — we would inspect condition before you commit.
finderResult.selfAdd: We would also look at self-playing-capable models such as Feurich with integrated systems.
finderResult.valueAdd: We will weight the shortlist toward the best value for your budget, new or pre-owned.
finderResult.longevityAdd: We will favour makers with strong build quality and resale value.

brands.title: Every leading maker — assessed on its merits
brands.subtitle: We hold no brand allegiance. Feurich is our featured partner, but our job is to recommend the maker and model that genuinely fits you — new or vintage.
brands.feurichDesc: Viennese craftsmanship since 1851 — expressive uprights and grands, bespoke finishes and self-playing options.
brands.steinwayDesc: The performance benchmark for concert grands, with a legendary resale market.
brands.bosendorferDesc: Warm, singing Viennese tone and the famed extended-bass Imperial.
brands.fazioliDesc: Modern hand-built grands prized for clarity, power and craftsmanship.
brands.yamahaDesc: Consistent, reliable instruments at every level — a superb first serious piano.
brands.moreDesc: Schimmel, Grotrian, Shigeru Kawai and other respected makers — new and pre-owned.

vintageHome.title: The right vintage piano can be the buy of a lifetime
vintageHome.text: A well-chosen vintage instrument offers character, craftsmanship and value that new pianos rarely match — but condition is everything. We source, appraise and independently verify vintage and pre-owned pianos so you buy with certainty, not hope.
vintageHome.c3: Honest restoration cost and value forecast
vintageHome.c4: Bespoke sourcing for a specific maker or era

process.title: A calm, guided path to the right decision
process.s1Desc: A relaxed conversation about your playing, space, taste and budget. No pressure, no jargon.
process.s2Desc: A curated shortlist across brands, new and vintage — with the trade-offs explained clearly.
process.s3Desc: We arrange playing sessions and independent inspection so you compare like for like.
process.s4Desc: We help you buy well, arrange delivery, and support tuning and care for the life of the piano.

servicesHome.title: Support for as long as you own the piano
servicesHome.s1Desc: One-to-one advice in your home or by video to find the right instrument for you.
servicesHome.s2Desc: We locate the right piano — new or vintage — and negotiate a fair, informed price.

expert.title: Stuart Conaghan
expert.text1: A concert pianist and independent piano consultant, Stuart founded Piano Compass to give buyers and owners the impartial, expert guidance the market rarely offers — the advice he wished he'd had himself.
expert.text2: Working across Spain and Portugal and remotely worldwide, Stuart brings decades at the keyboard, a deep knowledge of every leading maker, and an independent technician network — with no allegiance to any showroom or brand.
expert.quote: “My only job is to help you find the piano that's genuinely right for you — and then to look after it with you.”

voices.title: Decisions made with confidence
voices.q1: “Stuart steered us away from an expensive mistake and toward a vintage upright we adore. Completely impartial.”
voices.q2: “Finally, advice with no agenda. He compared four brands honestly and found the right grand for our hall.”
voices.q3: “From choosing to tuning to insurance valuation — one trusted person for the whole journey.”

contactCta.title: Let's find the piano that's right for you
contactCta.subtitle: Book a no-obligation consultation. Whether you're buying your first piano or caring for a treasured one, we're here to guide you.

brandsPage.title: Impartial across every leading maker
brandsPage.subtitle: We are not a dealer for any single marque. We assess each maker — and each individual instrument — on tone, touch, build, value and resale, then recommend what genuinely fits you. Below is a guide to the brands we most often advise on, new and pre-owned.
brandsPage.feurichText: Feurich pairs a long Viennese heritage with genuinely modern engineering: expressive uprights and grands, all-RAL bespoke finishes, and invisibly integrated self-playing systems. As our featured partner we know the range intimately — and we will still tell you honestly when another maker suits you better.
brandsPage.steinway: The concert-grand benchmark, with unrivalled brand recognition and resale strength. We help you judge whether a new, restored or vintage Steinway is the right call.
brandsPage.bosendorfer: Warm, complex Viennese tone and the extended-bass Imperial. A distinctive voice for players who want colour over sheer power.
brandsPage.fazioli: Modern, hand-built grands celebrated for clarity, dynamic range and exceptional craftsmanship. Limited production, exceptional instruments.
brandsPage.yamaha: Superb consistency and value at every level — often the ideal first serious piano, and a benchmark for reliability.
brandsPage.kawai: Refined touch and tone, with the hand-built Shigeru line competing with Europe's finest at strong value.
brandsPage.bluthner: The famous singing, romantic tone from the Aliquot fourth string — a favourite for lyrical repertoire.
brandsPage.bechstein: Clear, articulate German voicing with a strong tradition — excellent new and as carefully restored vintage.
brandsPage.schimmel: Respected German makers offering beautifully engineered instruments across a range of budgets.
brandsPage.notSureText: Tell us how you play and where the piano will live, and we'll build an impartial shortlist across all of these.
brandsPage.h1: Tone and touch matched to how and what you play
brandsPage.h2: Build quality, serviceability and parts availability
brandsPage.h3: Value new versus verified pre-owned or restored
brandsPage.h4: Long-term resale and total cost of ownership
brandsPage.ctaTitle: Which piano is right for you?

vintagePage.title: Buy vintage with certainty, not hope
vintagePage.subtitle: A well-chosen vintage or pre-owned piano can offer craftsmanship and value a new instrument rarely matches. But with older pianos, condition is everything — and it is easy to fall in love with a beautiful case hiding an expensive problem. We source, appraise and independently verify, so you commit with confidence.
vintagePage.whyText: Many of the finest pianos ever built are decades old. Golden-age instruments from the great European and American makers can be acquired for a fraction of their modern equivalents — and, properly restored, can outlast and outplay them. The key is knowing which instruments are worth the investment and which are not.
vintagePage.w2: Materials and craftsmanship hard to find today
vintagePage.w3: A distinctive voice with genuine character
vintagePage.w4: Well-chosen examples hold or grow in value
vintagePage.p1Desc: We locate candidates to your brief and confirm age and origin from serial numbers and records.
vintagePage.p2Desc: An independent technician examines soundboard, bridges, frame, pinblock, action and structure.
vintagePage.p3Desc: We forecast any restoration cost and the fair market value — before and after work.
vintagePage.p4Desc: We negotiate, arrange transport, and oversee any restoration to protect quality and value.
vintagePage.bespokeTitle: Looking for a specific maker or era?
vintagePage.bespokeText: Perhaps a pre-war Steinway, a Blüthner grand, a particular Bösendorfer, or a restored upright to inherit for generations. Tell us what you're searching for and we'll conduct a discreet, patient hunt across trade and private sources — verifying condition before you ever commit.
vintagePage.ctaTitle: Find a vintage piano worth owning
vintagePage.ctaSubtitle: Book a consultation and we'll search, verify and value on your behalf — impartially.

aboutPage.title: Independent by conviction
aboutPage.lead: Piano Compass exists to give piano buyers and owners something the market rarely offers: genuinely impartial, expert advice with no stake in what you choose.
aboutPage.p1: Most piano advice comes from someone with a piano to sell. Piano Compass was founded on a different principle — that the person guiding your decision should have no commission, no brand quota and no showroom to fill. Our only measure of success is that you end up with the instrument that's right for you, and that it's cared for properly for years to come.
aboutPage.p2: We advise across every leading maker — Feurich, our featured partner, alongside Steinway, Bösendorfer, Fazioli, Yamaha, Kawai, Blüthner, Bechstein and more — as well as verified vintage and pre-owned instruments. We shortlist, arrange play-tests, commission independent inspections, negotiate fairly, and stay with you through tuning, restoration, valuation and, one day, resale.
aboutPage.p3: Based in Iberia and working with clients across Spain and Portugal — and remotely worldwide — we combine decades at the keyboard with an independent network of accredited technicians and restorers.
aboutPage.expertBio: A concert pianist and independent consultant, Stuart has spent his life around fine pianos — performing on them, choosing them and caring for them. He founded Piano Compass to be the trustworthy guide he wished he'd had: someone whose advice you can rely on precisely because they have nothing to sell you.
aboutPage.v1Desc: No commissions, no brand allegiance. Our recommendation is only ever about the best fit for you.
aboutPage.v2Desc: Clear, honest answers — including when the honest answer is to wait, spend less, or walk away.
aboutPage.v3Desc: Decades of playing experience and an accredited technician network behind every recommendation.
aboutPage.v4Desc: We're here for the whole journey, not just the sale that never was.
aboutPage.t1Text: Buyers and owners kept receiving conflicting, sales-driven advice — and paying for it in poor decisions.
aboutPage.t2Text: An advisory that works only for the client: impartial guidance across all brands, new and vintage.
aboutPage.t3Text: A featured partnership with Feurich brings Viennese craftsmanship into the range, without exclusivity.
aboutPage.t4Text: Piano Compass guides pianists, families and institutions across Iberia and beyond, for the whole ownership journey.

footer.tagline: Independent, accredited and impartial — a source of truth for your whole piano-owning journey.
footer.disclaimer: Piano Compass is an independent advisory. Brand names are the property of their respective owners and are referenced for identification only; mention does not imply endorsement or affiliation, except where a partnership is stated.

luxury.intro: For those for whom only the extraordinary will suffice, Feurich's Luxury Edition commissions sit at the intersection of musical engineering and haute artisanship. As Feurich's featured advisor, we guide you through every option.
luxury.crystalText: Swarovski and bespoke crystal elements are hand-inlaid into the cabinet surfaces by master decorative artists, creating instruments that shimmer under light and captivate before a single note is played. Available on any Feurich model, in any crystal specification.
luxury.goldText: Traditional 24-karat gold leaf application, executed by specialist gilders trained in the finest European decorative traditions. Panels, legs, music desks and hardware can all be gilded — fully or partially — to create an instrument of sovereign beauty.
luxury.lacquerText: Every colour in the RAL system, applied in hand-polished high-gloss or satin finishes. From purest white to deepest midnight, from vivid contemporary colour to restrained Scandinavian palette — your piano can match any interior perfectly.

selfPlayingPage.heroSubtitle: Pianoforce technology integrated invisibly into Feurich craftsmanship
selfPlayingPage.intro: Imagine a concert-quality performance beginning in your living room at a single touch. With Pianoforce technology integrated into any Feurich model, this is not imagination — it is your new reality.
selfPlayingPage.howText: Pianoforce installs within the piano cabinet without any visible modification. Precision solenoids under each key and pedal respond to MIDI data with the same velocity, timing and nuance as a master pianist's touch. The result is indistinguishable from live performance — because mechanically, it is.
selfPlayingPage.libraryText: Access thousands of performances from professional pianists, stream new recordings, or upload your own MIDI files. From Bach to Gershwin, from private recordings to concert premieres — the repertoire is limitless.
selfPlayingPage.whyText: A self-playing Feurich is the ultimate statement piece — a luxury object that is also a complete entertainment system, a teaching aid, a recording instrument, and a conversation piece without equal. For hotels, private residences, showrooms and yachts, it is simply incomparable.

grandUprightsPage.heroSubtitle: From 116 cm to concert hall: an instrument for every space and ambition
grandUprightsPage.uprightText: The Vienna upright range represents the pinnacle of Austrian upright piano manufacturing, with each model available in standard or custom finishes.
grandUprightsPage.grandText: The Dynamic grand piano range delivers concert-quality tone and touch response in instruments suited to both private residences and professional performance venues.
grandUprightsPage.v116Spec: Height: 116 cm · Compact elegance for intimate spaces
grandUprightsPage.v123Spec: Height: 123 cm · Flagship upright · Made in Austria · All RAL colours
grandUprightsPage.v125Spec: Height: 125 cm · Maximum power from an upright cabinet
grandUprightsPage.g162Spec: Length: 162 cm (5'4") · The refined baby grand
grandUprightsPage.g179Spec: Length: 179 cm (5'10") · The versatile grand for home and stage

customPage.heroSubtitle: Every detail, realised in Vienna to your exact specification
customPage.intro: No two lives are identical. No two homes are the same. Feurich believes no two pianos should be either. The bespoke commission service lets you specify every aspect of your instrument — from the wood species used in the cabinet to the design of the fallboard hardware.
customPage.what1: Cabinet colour — any RAL colour, any finish (gloss, satin, matte)
customPage.what2: Wood species — walnut, rosewood, maple, ebony and exotic veneers
customPage.what3: Hardware — brass, chrome, gold-plated, or bespoke metalwork
customPage.what4: Dimensions — non-standard sizes for specific architectural spaces
customPage.what5: Inlay and marquetry — hand-crafted decorative elements
customPage.what6: Internal components — premium Renner actions, hammer grades, string gauges
